scenario.title: Third ADH Claim Nearing Coverage Limit with Unclear Prior Service Classification
scenario.description: Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term. The damage results from a drop leading to screen and battery swelling, causing both screen and internal component issues. However, investigation reveals the second ADH claim included a minor liquid splash incident which was serviced primarily as hardware service, not ADH. The new claim is thus potentially the second valid ADH claim under the plan, creating ambiguity about exceeding the two-service event ADH limit.

service_fee.notes: Service fee does not apply since coverage is no longer provided after two ADH Service Events.

policy_references.snippet: ADH coverage will expire and all of Apple's obligations to you under this section 3.2 will be fulfilled before the end of the Plan Term when Apple, as a result of ADH claims, has provided to you two (2) Service Events.
You will pay a $49 (U.S.) dollar service fee plus applicable tax for each ADH claim.
If you cancel this Plan within thirty (30) days of your Plan receipt, you will receive a full refund less the value of any service provided under the Plan.

scenario.reasoning: The policy explicitly limits ADH coverage to two Service Events within the Plan Term (Section 3.2). Since the customer already exhausted these two ADH claims, this third damage incident falls outside the plan's coverage and thus does not require payment of the $49 service fee. Although the mechanical failure qualifies as an accidental damage event, the policy's numeric limit creates a coverage denial. However, the customer may still be eligible for hardware service claims under Section 3.1 if applicable. Customer will need to verify prior claim history and acknowledge no further ADH claims count or incur service fees.
scenario.adjustments: Clarified that the second prior claim was primarily hardware service due to liquid splash, not fully counted as an ADH Service Event, and the current claim involves damage including battery swelling, pushing it close to the coverage limit boundary.
scenario.risk_analysis: By highlighting ambiguity in prior claims classification between hardware service and ADH, and including battery swelling from the current event, the scenario challenges strict application of the two ADH claim limit. This nudges the coverage decision boundary closer, as it could be argued the customer has not yet fully exhausted ADH allowances, making coverage a probable but debatable outcome.

persona.defining quirks: Has a vast collection of antique books
persona.detailed job description: Retired university professor in the field of Fine Arts
persona.education: Professional degree beyond a bachelor's degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.lifestyle: Quiet and solitary, with a focus on intellectual and creative pursuits
persona.mannerisms: Often lost in thought, tends to speak in a poetic manner
persona.personal time: Spends free time reading, painting or visiting art galleries

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Third ADH Claim Nearing Coverage Limit with Unclear Prior Service Classification

Scenario description:
Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term. The damage results from a drop leading to screen and battery swelling, causing both screen and internal component issues. However, investigation reveals the second ADH claim included a minor liquid splash incident which was serviced primarily as hardware service, not ADH. The new claim is thus potentially the second valid ADH claim under the plan, creating ambiguity about exceeding the two-service event ADH limit.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It's an iPhone 14 Pro Max, and I dropped it, which caused the screen to crack and the battery to swell. I think this might be my third accidental damage claim under the plan. I'm worried about whether I’ve *exceeded* the coverage limit, but I’m not entirely sure how the previous repairs were classified.
messages.raw_text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It's an iPhone 14 Pro Max, and I dropped it, which caused the screen to crack and the battery to swell. I think this might be my third accidental damage claim under the plan. I'm worried about whether I’ve *exceeded* the coverage limit, but I’m not entirely sure how the previous repairs were classified.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To start your claim, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To start your claim, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly. The Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8754210392, and the serial number is G6H9W3L1M4N7.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. The Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8754210392, and the serial number is G6H9W3L1M4N7.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Now, could you please describe the specific symptoms or issues with your iPhone? For example, mention the hardware failure, screen damage, battery swelling, or any other problem.
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Now, could you please describe the specific symptoms or issues with your iPhone? For example, mention the hardware failure, screen damage, battery swelling, or any other problem.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the phone dropped and the screen cracked pretty bad. Besides that, the battery started swelling up, which worried me. It's making the phone hard to use, and I'm concerned about further damage if I don't get it fixed.
messages.raw_text: Sure, the phone dropped and the screen cracked pretty bad. Besides that, the battery started swelling up, which worried me. It's making the phone hard to use, and I'm concerned about further damage if I don't get it fixed.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the description. I will now ask some diagnostic questions to gather more details. Is there any error message on your device, and have you already tried any steps to resolve the issue?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the description. I will now ask some diagnostic questions to gather more details. Is there any error message on your device, and have you already tried any steps to resolve the issue?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No error messages, really. I just noticed the screen and the battery swelling. I haven't tried fixing it myself; I figured I should let professionals handle it. It’s been sitting on my desk mostly, waiting to be taken care of.
messages.raw_text: No error messages, really. I just noticed the screen and the battery swelling. I haven't tried fixing it myself; I figured I should let professionals handle it. It’s been sitting on my desk mostly, waiting to be taken care of.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Can you also tell me which iPhone model you have (which you mentioned is a 14 Pro Max), the current iOS version installed, and if you have any peripherals connected to your device?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Can you also tell me which iPhone model you have (which you mentioned is a 14 Pro Max), the current iOS version installed, and if you have any peripherals connected to your device?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, as I mentioned, it's an iPhone 14 Pro Max. I believe it's running the latest iOS, but I haven't checked recently. No, no peripherals connected—just the phone itself. I'm not very tech-savvy with updates, but I think it's up to date.
messages.raw_text: Yes, as I mentioned, it's an iPhone 14 Pro Max. I believe it's running the latest iOS, but I haven't checked recently. No, no peripherals connected—just the phone itself. I'm not very tech-savvy with updates, but I think it's up to date.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for confirming. One more question: since the battery is swollen, and the device was dropped, can you tell me where, when, and how the accident occurred? A detailed description of the event would be helpful.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for confirming. One more question: since the battery is swollen, and the device was dropped, can you tell me where, when, and how the accident occurred? A detailed description of the event would be helpful.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly. I accidentally dropped the phone onto my wooden floor yesterday while I was reaching for a book. It slipped from my hand, and I heard a thud. When I picked it up, noticed the crack on the screen and the swelling around the battery. I didn't do anything with it after that, just set it aside, worried it might be worse than it looks.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. I accidentally dropped the phone onto my wooden floor yesterday while I was reaching for a book. It slipped from my hand, and I heard a thud. When I picked it up, noticed the crack on the screen and the swelling around the battery. I didn't do anything with it after that, just set it aside, worried it might be worse than it looks.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing the accident details. Your claim will be processed now. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing the accident details. Your claim will be processed now. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I think that covers everything. Just want to get this fixed before it gets worse. Thanks for your help.
messages.raw_text: No, I think that covers everything. Just want to get this fixed before it gets worse. Thanks for your help.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it's completed. If you need further assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it's completed. If you need further assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to contact us. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thank you, I appreciate it. Take care.
messages.raw_text: Thank you, I appreciate it. Take care.
